title: oxytropis adamsiana subsp. janensis
intro: Oxytropis adamsiana subsp. janensis, also known as Jan's globeflower, is a perennial plant species within the Fabaceae family. It is commonly found in the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and is used in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, and has been reported to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and exerting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, Oxytropis adamsiana subsp. janensis is often used in the form of extracts, teas, or infusions, and is also used in traditional Tibetan medicine to treat a range of ailments.
